summary: DHH shares a lighthearted personal anecdote about his girlfriend leaving for five months to study in Hong Kong and China. Within a week, his apartment has devolved into stereotypical bachelor chaos with pizza boxes and an empty fridge. He acknowledges his predictability and jokes about bribing his younger brother with Counter-Strike sessions in exchange for doing the dishes.
key_points: DHH's girlfriend left for five months, and domestic order immediately collapsed
He's self-aware about falling into bachelor stereotype clichés
He plans to recruit his 17-year-old brother for chores in exchange for gaming time
money_quotes: The pizza box tower has already taken over one of the two tables in the kitchen, the fridge is stripped of TV dinners, and I'm all out of milk, juice, and soda.
I am so predictable. And I fear it's only going to get worse.
What a wonderful thing that child labor isn't illegal if done within the family.
key_insight: A brief, self-deprecating slice-of-life post from early DHH blogging, long before his reputation as a tech provocateur.
